title: Two branches in a conditional structure should avoid having exactly the same implementation
description: Two branches in a conditional structure should not have exactly the same implementationAnalyze your codeadaptability-distinctmaintainabilityCode SmelldesignsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen the same code is duplicated in two or more separate branches of a conditional, it can make the code harder to understand, maintain, and can potentially introduce bugs if one instance of the code is changed but others are not.Having twocasesin aswitchstatement or two branches in anifchain with the same implementation is at best duplicate code, and at worst a coding error.if (a >= 0 && a < 10) { doFirstThing(); doTheThing(); } else if (a >= 10 && a < 20) { doTheOtherThing(); } else if (a >= 20 && a < 50) { doFirstThing(); doTheThing(); // Noncompliant; duplicates first condition } else { doTheR
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Two branches in a conditional structure should avoid having exactly the same implementation. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
